name: Bean and Rice Casserole
recipeIngredient: 1 can (15.25 - 19 oz) red kidney beans, drained and rinsed
1 can (15-16 oz) black beans, drained and rinsed
1 can (15-19 oz) garbanzo beans, drained and rinsed
1 can (14.5 - 16 oz) canned stewed tomatoes, drained
1 can (4 oz) chopped mild chiles, drained
10 ounces frozen green peas, thawed by placing under running water
recipeInstructions:
2. Preheat oven to 375.
3. In a 5-quart Dutch oven over medium-high heat, sauté onion in liquid of your choice (balsamic vinegar gets my vote) until tender, stirring occasionally. Add rice and cook while stirring until parched and opaque.
4. Add beans, tomatoes, chiles, and 3.5 cups of water to rice and onion in Dutch oven and bring mixture to a boil.
5. Cover tightly and place in oven, baking until rice is tender and liquid is absorbed (around 1 hour for brown rice).
6. Add peas and corn, adjust seasonings, and return to oven until heated through.
If using no-salt-added tomatoes, reserve the drained liquid and add water to equal the 3.5 cups of liquid.
I add Tabasco sauce to make it a bit spicy.
